steps.title: Find a counterexample
steps.content: The student's prediction is that bird species with mean body masses greater than 150 grams do not use deceptive defenses. To weaken it, the table must show a bird above 150 grams that still performs broken-wing display. The example must cross the threshold and still show the deceptive behavior named in the prediction about larger birds.

steps.title: Reject noncounterexamples
steps.content: Choice A says the blue-winged teal does not use broken-wing display, which would not weaken the prediction. Choice B compares two birds without crossing the 150-gram threshold. Choice C uses only birds under 150 grams. The answer is D.
